mathura_places.name: Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi Temple
mathura_places.address: Krishna Janmasthan, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: The holiest site in Mathura, believed to be the exact birthplace of Lord Krishna in King Kansa's prison. The complex houses the main Keshavdeva Temple and the sacred underground prison cell (Garbha Griha). During Janmashtami, millions of devotees gather here for spectacular midnight celebrations marking Krishna's birth.

mathura_places.name: Dwarkadhish Temple
mathura_places.address: Dwarkadhish Temple, Vishram Ghat Road, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: One of the most revered temples in Mathura, dedicated to Lord Krishna as king of Dwarka. Built in 1814 by Seth Gokuldas Parikh, the temple features stunning Rajasthani-style architecture with intricate carvings and vibrant paintings. The Swing Festival (Jhulan Yatra) and Holi celebrations here are legendary.

mathura_places.name: Vishram Ghat
mathura_places.address: Vishram Ghat, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: The most sacred ghat in Mathura, where Lord Krishna is believed to have rested after slaying the tyrant King Kansa. The evening Yamuna Aarti here is a mesmerising spectacle of oil lamps, chanting, and devotion. A boat ride from Vishram Ghat reveals 25 beautiful ghats lining the Yamuna riverbank.

mathura_places.name: Government Museum Mathura
mathura_places.address: Government Museum, Dampier Nagar, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: One of the finest archaeological museums in India, housing an outstanding collection of sculptures from the ancient Mathura School of Art (Kushan and Gupta periods). The museum's prized collection includes rare Buddha and Jain statues, terracotta artefacts, coins, and inscriptions dating back over 2,000 years.

mathura_places.name: Kans Qila
mathura_places.address: Kans Qila, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: An ancient fort on the banks of the Yamuna, believed to be the palace-fort of the tyrannical King Kansa. Though largely in ruins, the massive walls and strategic riverside location evoke the epic confrontation between Krishna and his maternal uncle. The site offers panoramic views of the Yamuna and surrounding ghats.

mathura_places.name: Gita Mandir
mathura_places.address: Gita Mandir, Mathura-Vrindavan Road, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: Also known as Birla Mandir, this beautiful temple built by the Birla family features the entire Bhagavad Gita inscribed on its pillars and walls. The red sandstone structure sits amidst well-maintained gardens and is a popular stop on the Mathura-Vrindavan route. The temple's serene atmosphere makes it ideal for contemplation.

mathura_places.name: Bhuteshwar Mahadev Temple
mathura_places.address: Bhuteshwar Mahadev Temple, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: An ancient and deeply revered Shiva temple, considered one of the most important Shiva shrines in the Braj region. The temple draws large crowds during Shravan month and Maha Shivratri. The sacred linga here is believed to be self-manifested (swayambhu), and devotees come from across North India for darshan.

mathura_places.name: Potra Kund
mathura_places.address: Potra Kund, near Krishna Janmasthan, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: A sacred ancient step-well (kund) located near the Krishna Janmabhoomi complex. According to tradition, this is where baby Krishna's clothes were washed after his birth. The beautifully preserved sandstone steps descend to the water level, and the kund remains an important pilgrimage site visited by devotees year-round.

mathura_places.name: Tilak Dwar Market
mathura_places.address: Tilak Dwar, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: A vibrant market area near the main temples, famous for Mathura's legendary pedas (milk sweets), puja items, and religious souvenirs. The narrow lanes are lined with sweet shops that have been crafting Mathura ke pedas for generations. Also a great place to buy brass items, bangles, and traditional Braj handicrafts.

mathura_places.name: Chowk Bazaar
mathura_places.address: Chowk Bazaar, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: The bustling heart of old Mathura, Chowk Bazaar is a lively market known for its traditional sweets, street food, and festive atmosphere. During Holi, this area transforms into one of the most colourful celebrations in India. The bazaar offers an authentic glimpse into Braj's daily life and culture.

mathura_places.name: Brijwasi Mithai
mathura_places.address: Brijwasi Mithai, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: The most iconic sweet shop in Mathura, established over a century ago. Famous for its melt-in-your-mouth Mathura Pedas — golden milk sweets made with pure khoya, sugar, and saffron. No visit to Mathura is complete without tasting their legendary pedas, kachoris, and lassi.

mathura_places.name: Om Sweets Mathura
mathura_places.address: Om Sweets, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: A popular sweet and snack shop known for its wide variety of traditional Braj sweets, namkeens, and chaat. A favourite among locals and visitors, Om Sweets offers an authentic taste of Mathura's culinary heritage at reasonable prices.

mathura_places.name: Jama Masjid Mathura
mathura_places.address: Jama Masjid, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
mathura_places.description: A historic mosque built during the Mughal period by Aurangzeb's governor Abd-un-Nabi Khan. The mosque's four minarets and large courtyard reflect Mughal architectural style. It stands adjacent to the Krishna Janmasthan complex, representing the layered religious history of Mathura.
